revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:五條天神社 (台東区)]][[ko:고조텐 신사]][[zh:五条天神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Gojoten-Jinja.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Ueno-kōen|qid=Q92909922|1=Ueno-kōen}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.71375|N|139.77211111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Q11622504|1=Three Medicine Gods|2=ja|3=薬祖神}}}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = {{ill|Medical deity Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical deity Gojō Tenjin Shrine|1=Gojō Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372505}} |image = [[File:Gojoten-Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|49.5|N|139|46|19.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|qid=Q746216|lt=Ueno Onshi Park|1=Ueno Park}} 4-17 |Latitude_deg = 35 |Latitude_min = 42 |Latitude_sec = 49.5 | N |Longitude_deg = 139 |Longitude_min = 46 |Longitude_sec = 19.6 | E |Map Country Code = JP-13 |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Ōnamuji no Mikoto|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuji no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q276944}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona no Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |type = |Founded = Traditionally during the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Emperor|1=Emperor Keikō}} era (circa 110 AD) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |festival = {{ill|May 25|en|May 25|qid=Q2585|lt=May 25|1=May 25}} |Rituals = }} '''Gojō Tenjin Shrine''' (五條天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|qid=Q746216|lt=Ueno Onshi Park|1=Ueno Park}}, {{ill|Taitō|en|Taitō|qid=Q232641|lt=Taitō|1=Taitō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. A branch shrine of the Kyoto location.{{Cite web|title=The Origins of Yakusojin|publisher=Tokyo Pharmaceutical Association|url=http://www.yakujikyo.or.jp/festival/history.html|accessdate=2019-10-12|en}} It enshrines the {{ill|Medical deity|ja|薬祖神|1=Three Medicine Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Medicine Gods|qq=}}, but also serves as a {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}}. Additionally, it is associated with {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, as it manages the adjacent '''Hanazono Inari Shrine''' (花園稲荷神社), though this shrine is not part of its precincts. == Deities == * '''Main Deities''' ''' {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (Ōnamuji no Mikoto) ''' {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) * '''deity in the Joint Hall''' ''' {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} (Sugawara no Michizane) == Overview == === History === According to shrine tradition, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} enshrined Ōnamuji no Mikoto and Sukunahikona no Mikoto in {{ill|Shinobazu Hill|ja|忍が岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobazu Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during his {{ill|Eastern expedition of Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル#東征|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern expedition of Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}. The shrine is also mentioned in '''1486''' in the travel record *Hokkokuki* by '''Gyōe'''{{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}}{{Sfn|Edo Meisho Zue. Vol.3 (Gojō Tenjin-gū)|1927|p=474}}. It is confirmed that the shrine was in '''Ueno Mountain''' by the '''mid-Muromachi period'''Sign at the old site of Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Taitō City Board of Education).. However, due to the expansion of '''{{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|qid=Q3120766|lt=Kaneiji|1=Kan'ei-ji}}''', the shrine relocated multiple timesShrine precincts sign.: - Originally on '''Tenjin Hill''' (now '''Suri-bachi Hill''', near '''Kiyomizu Kannon-dō'''){{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}} - Moved near the '''Black Gate of Kaneiji''' (now the southern end of Ueno Park) * '''1697 (Genroku 10)''': Relocated to '''Segawa Residence''' (now the entrance to '''Ameya-Yokochō'''){{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}} * '''1928 (Shōwa 3)''': Returned to its present location near its original site{{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}} In '''1641 (Kan'ei 18)''', '''Tenkai''' and '''Kōkai (monk)|Kōkai''' enshrined an image of '''Sugawara no Michizane''', leading to its designation as '''Shitaya Tenmangū''' (下谷天満宮), a shrine revered for poetry and literature{{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}}. === Edo Period === The '''1662 (Kanbun 2)''' *Edo Meisho-ki* mentions '''Gyōe''' restoring the shrine as "Ueno’s Guardian" near the '''Black Gate of Kaneiji''', calling it '''Ushi Tenjin (Ox Tenjin)'''. It also describes '''Shinobazu Inari''' at '''Shinobazu Hill'''{{Sfn|Edo Sōsho Vol. 12|1916|pp=13-15}}. The '''1793 (Kansei 5)''' *Sugawara no Michizane's Correspondence* identifies '''Shitaya Tenjin Shrine''' as part of '''Gojō Tenjin Shrine''', noting that it enshrines '''Sukunahikona no Mikoto'''{{Sfn|Sugawara no Michizane Correspondence|1817|pp=16-17}}. The '''1834 (Tenpō 5)''' *Edo Meisho Zue* describes "Gojō Tenjin-gū," stating it was moved from the '''Kaneiji Honbō area''' to the '''Segawa Residence'''. It lists '''Sukunahikona no Mikoto''' as the main deity and '''Kitano Tenmangū''' as a joint deity, with the '''Sugawara no Michizane statue consecrated in 1641''' by '''Tenkai'''{{Sfn|Edo Meisho Zue. Vol.3 (Gojō Tenjin-gū)|1927|pp=471-474}}. During the Edo period, it was one of the '''Seven Great Tenjin Shrines of Edo''' and sometimes included in the '''Three Great Tenjin Shrines of Edo''' and the '''25 Tenjin Shrines of Edo'''. After relocating to the Segawa Residence, the area became '''"Gojō Tenjin Monzen"''', later renamed '''"Gojō-chō"''' in '''1869 (Meiji 2)'''. The district was absorbed into '''Ueno''' in '''1964 (Shōwa 39)'''. In '''2010 (Heisei 22)''', a grand festival was held to commemorate the shrine’s '''1900th anniversary''', moving the '''three-yearly portable shrine procession (2011) forward'''. == Hanazono Inari Shrine == [[File:Hanazozo Inari Jinja 05 (15562358337).jpg|thumb|Hanazono Inari Shrine Haiden]] ; '''Hanazono Inari Shrine''' :* '''deity:''' {{ill|Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080728}} (alt. name: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime|1=Toyouke-hime}}) : The exact founding date is unknown, but it predates the arrival of Gojō Tenjin Shrine{{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}}. One legend states it was brought from '''Mount Inari''' by '''Kūkai''', another attributes it to '''Tenkai''', while others say it was restored by '''Mokujiki Jōun'''{{Sfn|Edo Meisho Zue. Vol.3 (Shinobazu Inari Shrine)|1927|p=240}}. The shrine was restored in '''1654 (Jōō 3)''' by '''Kōkai''', a disciple of Tenkai{{Sfn|Gojō Tenjin Shrine}}. : During the '''Ueno War''', it was a battlefield near the '''Black Gate and Ana-Inari Gate'''. In '''1873 (Meiji 6)''', it was renamed '''Hanazono Inari Shrine''' due to its location in Kaneiji’s flower fields. Despite being managed by Gojō Tenjin Shrine, it remains an '''independent religious corporation'''. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Gojō Tenjin-gū|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukutoku Shrine|ja|福徳神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukutoku Shrine|1=Fukutoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q118114706}} – Its subsidiary shrine, {{ill|Yakuso Shrine|ja|薬祖神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuso Shrine|1=Yakuso Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622505}}, originates from here. * {{ill|Medical Deities|ja|薬祖神|1=Three Medicine Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Medicine Gods|qq=}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū shrines|1=Tenmangū}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160919020754/http://www.gojotenjinja.jp/ Gojō Tenjin Shrine & Hanazono Inari Shrine] (Archived official website) {{shinto-stub}} == Notable Shrines == * {{ill|Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|五條天神社|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojo Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto)|1=Gojō Tenjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372505}} (Kyoto) - Features the "Isoshin Memorial Stone." * {{ill|Yakusojin Shrine (Sakai)|ja|薬祖神社 (堺市)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusojin Shrine (Sakai)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Osaka) - Claims to be the oldest Yakusojin shrine in Japan.{{Cite web|title=Sakai Tenjin and Yakusojin Shrines|url=http://www.sakaitenjin.or.jp/original3.html|accessdate=2019-10-12|en}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona Shrine (Osaka)|ja|少彦名神社 (大阪市)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona Shrine (Osaka)|1=Sukunahikona Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11464751}} (Osaka) - Known as "Doshomachi's Shennong Shrine." * {{ill|Yakusojin Shrine (Tokyo)|ja|薬祖神社 (東京都中央区)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusojin Shrine (Tokyo)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tokyo) - Relocated during redevelopment.{{Cite web|title=Rebirth of Fukutoku Forest|publisher=Mitsui Fudosan|url=https://www.mitsuifudosan.co.jp/corporate/news/2016/0928/|accessdate=2019-10-12|en}} * {{ill|Ikune Shrine (Sumiyoshi)|ja|生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikune Shrine (Sumiyoshi)|1=Ikune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574575}} (Osaka) - One of the oldest shrines dedicated solely to Sukuna Hikona.{{Cite book|title=Report on Shrines Vol. 5|author=Okuno Shigehisa|year=1977|publisher=Kogakkan University|ref={{Harvid|Ikune Shrine (Report on Shrines)|1977|en}}|en}} {{translated page|ja|五條天神社 (台東区)|version=101759330|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:五條天神社]] [[ko:%EA%B3%A0%EC%A1%B0%ED%85%90 %EC%8B%A0%EC%82%AC]] [[zh:%E4%BA%94%E6%9D%A1%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Commons category|Gojōten-jinja (Tokyo)}} [[ja:五條天神社 (台東区)]] [[ko:고조텐 신사]] [[zh:五条天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q2115862}} [[Category:Inari-jinja in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Taito, Tokyo]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Ueno Park]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
